posts.text: Raw transcripts are a starting point, not finished copy. Filler, false starts, unverified links, and corrected statements all end up in there — treat it as clean truth and every piece of content built from it inherits those problems. Clean your transcript before you build on it.

Most podcast production failures happen at the handoff, not the recording. Where does your production process lose the most time or create the most confusion — editing, writing, review, or publishing?
